zones.name: Ubud Cultural & Wellness Heart
zones.description: The spiritual and artistic soul of Bali, Ubud is famous worldwide for its ancient monkey forest sanctuary, royal palace, and a thriving wellness scene. This dense hub offers everything from traditional Balinese spa rituals and yoga retreats to vibrant art markets and cultural performances. It's one of Southeast Asia's premier destinations for holistic healing and immersive cultural experiences.

all_places.name: Mount batur sunrise Trekking and jeep Sunrise tour
Ôde à l'Indonésie - Art Photo Gallery by Marius Moragues
Heart Space Bali - Yin Yoga & Holistic Studio

mount batur sunrise trekking ( private tour )
Bali waterfall treking tours near Ubud

zones.name: Ubud Northern Retreats & Rice Terrace Villages
zones.description: Just north of central Ubud, this tranquil corridor winds through lush river valleys and boutique spa hideaways, including the world-famous Tegallalang Rice Terraces — a UNESCO-recognized landscape and one of Bali's most photographed sights. Eco-cycling tours through jungle paths and exclusive villa retreats make this a favourite for travellers seeking slower, more intimate Bali experiences.

top_places.name: Asvara Villa Keliki by iNi Vie Hospitality
Mekar Ubud Jungle Spa by K club

zones.name: Ubud South: Temples, Jungle Swings & Spas
zones.description: South of Ubud's centre, this zone blends spiritual heritage with adrenaline-fuelled jungle adventures, featuring the ancient Goa Gajah elephant cave temple dating back to the 9th century. Balinese cooking classes, yoga retreats like Soulshine Bali, and singing bowl sound healing ceremonies make this a uniquely multi-dimensional area. The riverside scenery and ricefield backdrops create a perfect backdrop for unforgettable experiences.

all_places.name: Gdas Bali Health and Wellness Resort
Museum Rudana & Rudana Fine Art Gallery
Samyama Self-Healing Center | Yoga & Meditation in Ubud, Bali
Ubud Diary | Bali Fine Art | Bali Art Gallery

zones.name: Ayung River Adventure Corridor
zones.description: This dramatic valley corridor along the sacred Ayung River — Bali's longest river — is the island's premier white-water rafting destination, attracting thousands of adventure seekers annually. Lush jungle canopy, hidden waterfalls, and ancient stone carvings line the riverbanks as you navigate the thrilling rapids. The area also harbours some of Ubud's most secluded luxury hideaways and jungle wellness retreats.

top_places.name: Bali Tarantula Adventure - ATV , RAFTING AND SWING

all_places.name: Asvara Villa Keliki by iNi Vie Hospitality
Rudra Sahashrara Bali - Ayurvedic - Health and Wellness Resort

zones.name: Seminyak–Kerobokan: Bali's Trendiest Beachfront Strip
zones.description: Bali's most glamorous coastal stretch is anchored by the iconic Potato Head Beach Club, one of Asia's most-reviewed beach clubs with nearly 23,000 ratings. This sophisticated zone blends world-class dining, designer boutiques, buzzing rooftop bars, and high-end spas into a seamless beachfront lifestyle experience. It's the epicentre of Bali's luxury nightlife and fashion scene, drawing an international jet-set crowd year-round.

all_places.name: Krisna Oleh - Oleh Bali Sunset Road

zones.name: Canggu & Berawa: Surf, Beach Clubs & Digital Nomad Hub
zones.description: Canggu has transformed from a sleepy rice-field village into Bali's coolest neighbourhood for surfers, digital nomads, and beach-club enthusiasts. FINNS Beach Club reigns as one of Southeast Asia's largest beach clubs, drawing over 41,000 reviews, while surf schools, yoga shalas, and trendy cafes line every lane. The juxtaposition of ancient rice paddies and rooftop infinity pools creates Bali's most photographed lifestyle contrasts.

all_places.name: Astera Resort & Spa Canggu by iNi Vie Hospitality
Canggu Cabana Beach Resort & Spa by iNi Vie Hospitality

zones.name: Tanjung Benoa: Water Sports & Family Beach Resort
zones.description: Tanjung Benoa is Bali's dedicated water sports peninsula, stretching into Benoa Bay and famed for its calm, protected waters that are perfect for parasailing, jet-skiing, banana boats, and turtle island visits. The area hosts a cluster of large family-friendly resorts and is home to the Deluang Sari Turtle Conservation Center, where visitors can release baby turtles into the ocean. Its sheltered beach and variety of activities make it the top family water sports destination on the island.

top_places.name: Water Sport Adventure Bali Dolphin Shop

Water Sport Adventure Bali Dolphin Shop
Jetski Touring Fun Day Nusa Dua
Jetski Touring Fun Day Nusa Dua

zones.name: Nusa Dua: Luxury Resorts & Spa Enclave
zones.description: Nusa Dua is Bali's most prestigious resort enclave, purpose-built to house the island's grandest five-star properties including Mulia Resort and Ayodya Resort along an immaculate white-sand beach. Zahra Spa here is one of Bali's most-reviewed spas with over 31,000 ratings, a testament to the area's world-class pampering credentials. Gated, manicured, and serene, it's the top choice for luxury travellers seeking exclusivity, high-end dining, and pristine beach access.

all_places.name: Novotel Bali Nusa Dua Hotel & Residences
Nusa Dua Beach Hotel & Spa, Bali
The Laguna, a Luxury Collection Resort & Spa, Nusa Dua, Bali

zones.name: Jimbaran Bay: Sunset Seafood & Clifftop Resorts
zones.description: Jimbaran Bay is Bali's most romantic dining destination, world-famous for its row of beachfront seafood warungs where you dine on grilled fish with your feet in the sand as the sun melts into the Indian Ocean. Home to the iconic Four Seasons and InterContinental resorts, this crescent-shaped bay also marks the gateway to Bali's dramatic southern cliffs. AYANA Resort's Rock Bar — perched on natural limestone outcrops above the sea — is one of the most iconic sunset venues in all of Asia.

top_places.name: Four Seasons Resort Bali At Jimbaran Bay
Bawang Merah Beachfront Restaurant, Jimbaran Bay Seafood

all_places.name: Four Seasons Resort Bali At Jimbaran Bay
Jimbaran Puri, A Belmond Hotel, Bali

zones.name: Uluwatu & Ungasan: Clifftop Temples, Surf & Nightlife
zones.description: Perched on Bali's southernmost limestone cliffs, Uluwatu is home to the awe-inspiring Pura Uluwatu temple rising 70 metres above the crashing Indian Ocean — one of Bali's six most sacred temples and a venue for nightly Kecak fire dance performances at sunset. The area has evolved into a world-class surf and luxury destination, with OMNIA Bali and Savaya drawing international party-goers to cliff-edge infinity pools. Alchemy Yoga, boutique eco-resorts, and hidden cove beaches complete this extraordinary clifftop world.

top_places.name: Tropical Temptation - Adult Only Beach Club by Wonderspace
Alchemy Yoga and Meditation Center Uluwatu

zones.name: Tanah Lot: Iconic Temple & Tabanan Coast
zones.description: Tanah Lot is arguably Bali's single most photographed landmark — a 16th-century sea temple dramatically rising from a rocky islet in the Indian Ocean, drawing over 100,000 reviews and visited by millions each year. The surrounding Tabanan coastline offers a more authentic, less commercialised side of Bali with dramatic black-sand beaches and traditional farming villages. The nearby Taman Ayun Royal Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, adds further cultural gravitas to this legendary coastal corridor.

zones.name: Denpasar & Kuta: Shopping, Markets & City Life
zones.description: Denpasar, Bali's bustling capital, and adjacent Kuta together form the island's commercial and shopping heartland, anchored by the legendary Badung Market — Bali's largest traditional market operating since 1897. The Keranjang shopping mall and Krisna souvenir chains draw millions of shoppers annually, while night markets and textile shops cater to every taste and budget. This area pulses with authentic Balinese urban life, offering the best value dining, local snacks, and craft shopping on the island.

top_places.name: Krisna Toko Oleh Oleh Khas Bali
Paramatex - Toko Kain Terbaik di Bali

zones.name: Sanur: Family Beach, Night Markets & Laid-Back Coast
zones.description: Sanur is Bali's most family-friendly beach resort town, beloved for its calm reef-protected waters, charming tree-lined promenade, and relaxed pace that contrasts with the bustle of Kuta and Seminyak. The Sindhu Night Market and Pasar Sindu are beloved local food and craft markets, while Sanur's beach is the main departure point for fast boats to Nusa Penida and Nusa Lembongan. With boutique hotels, art galleries, and a colourful culinary scene, Sanur perfectly balances authenticity and comfort.

top_places.name: Bluuu Tours - Boat Tours And Rental Bali to Nusa Penida

zones.name: Gianyar & Sukawati: Craft Villages & Art Markets
zones.description: The Gianyar Regency corridor is the beating artistic heart of Bali, where generations-old craft villages specialise in silverwork, woodcarving, batik, and stone sculpture. Sukawati Art Market is one of Bali's oldest and most famous handicraft markets, a must-visit for affordable Balinese souvenirs, while the Pasar Senggol Gianyar night market is beloved by locals for authentic traditional foods. The Barong and Kris Dance performances here are among the best places to witness sacred Balinese performing arts.

zones.name: Bali Zoo, Safari & Family Adventure Belt
zones.description: Stretching through central Gianyar, this family entertainment belt is home to Bali Zoo, the Amazing Taman Safari Bali — one of Asia's largest drive-through wildlife parks — and Bali Bird Park, one of the world's top-rated tropical bird parks with over 1,000 exotic species. The area offers some of the most diverse family-friendly attractions on the island all within easy reach of each other. Bali Reptile Park rounds out an action-packed wildlife day that delights visitors of all ages.

zones.name: Pura Tirta Empul & Sacred Highland Temples
zones.description: The sacred spring temple of Pura Tirta Empul, built in 926 AD, is one of Bali's holiest sites where Balinese Hindus and curious travellers alike come to purify themselves in ancient ritual bathing pools fed by a natural spring. Nearby Pura Gunung Kawi Sebatu and the enigmatic Goa Gajah archaeological site form a remarkable trail of spiritual heritage sites through Bali's verdant central highlands. Farm cooking schools in this area are consistently rated among the best in Asia, offering unforgettable paddyfield-to-plate experiences.

top_places.name: Pemulan Bali Farm Cooking School, Ubud
Taman Dukuh Bali Farm Cooking School | Cooking Class Ubud

zones.name: Mount Batur: Volcano Sunrise Trekking & Kintamani
zones.description: Mount Batur is Bali's most active volcano and the island's most popular trekking destination, with thousands of hikers summiting before dawn each week to witness a legendary sunrise above the clouds and crater lake below. The Kintamani Caldera is one of Southeast Asia's most dramatic volcanic landscapes, and the area offers jeep adventures, hot spring soaks, and eco-village cycling routes. Sitting at 1,717 metres elevation, it remains one of Bali's most unforgettable natural experiences.

top_places.name: Mount batur sunrise trekking & private jeep tour
Mount Batur Sunrise Trekking & Jeep Adventure
Maitri Retreats - Silent meditation retreats in Bali and Portugal

all_places.name: Mount batur sunrise trekking & private jeep tour

zones.name: Bedugul & Beratan Highlands: Lake Temples & Botanical Gardens
zones.description: Set at 1,200 metres elevation amidst cool misty highlands, the Ulun Danu Beratan Temple is Bali's most iconic lake temple — a floating Shivaite pagoda reflected in the mirror-like waters of Lake Beratan and featured on Indonesia's 50,000-rupiah banknote. Kebun Raya Bali (Bali Botanical Garden) sprawls across 157 hectares nearby and is the largest botanical garden in Indonesia. This refreshingly cool escape from the tropical heat offers dramatic volcanic scenery and some of Bali's most striking photography opportunities.

zones.name: Tulamben: World-Class Wreck Diving
zones.description: Tulamben is one of the world's most celebrated dive sites, built around the legendary USAT Liberty shipwreck — a World War II cargo ship that now rests just 30 metres from shore in shallow waters, making it accessible even to novice divers. The black volcanic beach and extraordinarily rich marine biodiversity draw divers from every corner of the globe, with operators like Dive Concepts and Let's Dive Tulamben holding near-perfect ratings. This quiet village on Bali's northeast coast is a must-visit for anyone with any interest in underwater exploration.

top_places.name: Tulamben Scuba Dive & Snorkeling - Scuba Go Indonesia

all_places.name: Tulamben Scuba Dive & Snorkeling - Scuba Go Indonesia

zones.name: Amed: Snorkeling, Fishing Villages & Slow Travel
zones.description: Amed is a string of traditional fishing villages on Bali's dramatic northeast coast, known for its exceptional snorkelling straight off the black volcanic beach and a wonderfully unhurried pace of life. The coral reefs here teem with colourful marine life including turtles, lionfish, and reef sharks, drawing snorkellers who prefer unspoilt waters over tourist crowds. Multiple operators receive perfect 5-star ratings, reflecting the genuine warmth and professionalism of Amed's local boat crews.

top_places.name: AMED BEST SNORKELING TOUR |Diving Fishing And Sunset Trip

all_places.name: Amed Snorkeling Tour By Boat | AMED,BALI

zones.name: Nusa Penida & Nusa Lembongan: Island Escapes & Manta Rays
zones.description: Just a 30-minute fast boat from Sanur, Nusa Penida has exploded onto the global travel scene as one of Indonesia's hottest destinations, famous for its dramatic clifftop viewpoints at Kelingking Beach and the chance to swim with giant oceanic manta rays. Nusa Lembongan, its smaller sister island, offers a more chilled escape with mangrove forests, surf breaks, and boutique resorts. Together they form one of Southeast Asia's most rewarding island-hopping adventures accessible as a day trip or overnight stay.

top_places.name: Nusa Penida Spot Tour & Travel (META TOUR)
Tour Nusa Penida Daily | Manta Ray Snorkeling

all_places.name: Nusa Penida Spot Tour & Travel (META TOUR)
One Day Trip Nusa Penida - Nusapenidatourist.com

zones.name: Besakih & East Bali: Mother Temple & Ancient Heritage
zones.description: Pura Besakih — the Mother Temple of Bali — is the island's largest and holiest temple complex, perched dramatically on the slopes of Mount Agung at 1,000 metres elevation and comprising 23 separate temples that have been in continuous use for over 1,000 years. The East Bali regency is a treasure trove of ancient cultural heritage including the traditional Aga village of Tenganan Pegringsingan, one of Bali's oldest indigenous villages famed for its rare double-ikat textile weaving. The Asri Dining at Samanvaya offers an extraordinary farm-to-table culinary experience in the area.

zones.name: West Bali National Park & Menjangan Island
zones.description: West Bali National Park is the island's only national park, protecting 77,000 hectares of pristine wilderness including mangroves, savannah, and tropical forest that shelters the critically endangered Bali Starling — one of the world's rarest birds. Just offshore, Menjangan Island offers some of Bali's most spectacular wall diving, with dramatic coral drop-offs and crystal-clear visibility that rivals the best diving in Asia. The remote Banjar Hot Springs, fed by volcanic geothermal springs, provide a soothing natural bathing experience in this far-flung corner of the island.

top_places.name: Birding Tours in Bali ( Made Surya )
